content:
# 1 Peter Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), page 268 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1PE_004_page_001.jpg

1. Christos therefore having suffered in [the] flesh, you also arm yourselves [with] the same mind, because the [one] having suffered in [the] flesh has ceased from sin,

2. so as to live the remaining time in [the] flesh no longer by [the] desires of men but by [the] will of God.

3. For sufficient [is] the time having passed to have worked out the will of the nations, having walked in licentiousness, desires, drunkenness, revelries, drinking bouts, and unlawful idolatries,

4. in which they are surprised [that] you do not run together [with them] into the same flood of dissipation, blaspheming —

5. who will give account to the [one] having readiness to judge [the] living and [the] dead.

6. For unto this also to [the] dead [the] good news was proclaimed, that they might be judged according to men in [the] flesh, but might live according to God in [the] spirit.

7. But the end of all things has drawn near. Be sober-minded therefore and be watchful unto prayers,

8. before all things having the love among yourselves [being] fervent, because love covers a multitude of sins.

9. [Be] hospitable to one another without grumbling,

10. each [one] just as he received a gift, ministering it among yourselves as good stewards of [the] manifold grace of God.

11. If anyone speaks, [let it be] as oracles of God; if anyone ministers, [let it be] as from [the] strength which the God supplies, that in all things the God may be glorified through Yeshua Christos, to whom is the glory and the might unto the ages of the ages. Amen.

12. Beloved, do not be surprised at the burning among you which comes to you for [a] testing, as [though] a strange thing happening to you,

13. but insofar as you share in the sufferings of the Christos, rejoice, that also at the revelation of the glory of him you may rejoice, exulting.

14. If you are reproached in [the] name of Christos, blessed [are you], because the [Spirit] of the glory and the [Spirit] of the God upon you rests.

15. For let not any of you suffer as a murderer or a thief or an evildoer or as a meddler in others' affairs,

16. but if [one suffers] as a Christian, let him not be ashamed, but let him glorify the God in this name.

17. Because [it is] the time for the judgment to begin from the house of the God; and if first from us, what [will be] the end of the [ones] disobeying the good news of the God?

18. And if the righteous [one] is scarcely saved, the ungodly and sinful [one] — where will he appear?

19. So then, also let the [ones] suffering according to the will of the God entrust the souls of them to a faithful Creator in doing good.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **4:1** — Sinaiticus reads "Christos therefore having suffered" (Χριστου ουν παθοντος). The manuscript omits "for us" (υπερ ημων / υπερ υμων) found in many later manuscripts. EMET follows the Sinaiticus reading.

2. **4:1** — Sinaiticus reads "has ceased from sin" (πεπαυται αμαρτιας) — singular "sin" rather than "sins."

3. **4:3** — Sinaiticus reads "to have worked out" (κατειργασθαι) the will of the nations. The list of vices follows the standard catalog form. The term "licentiousness" renders ασελγειαις (dative plural).

4. **4:5** — Sinaiticus reads "having readiness" (ετοιμως εχοντι) to judge, i.e., the one who holds himself in readiness. This refers to God as the judge of living and dead.

5. **4:8** — Sinaiticus reads "the love among yourselves being fervent" (την αγαπην εκτενη εχοντες). The word εκτενη ("stretched out, fervent") conveys intensity and persistence.

6. **4:11** — Sinaiticus reads "the God" (ο θεος) with the article in both occurrences in this verse. The doxology "to whom is the glory and the might" (ω εστιν η δοξα και το κρατος) closes with the standard liturgical formula.

7. **4:14** — Sinaiticus reads "the [Spirit] of the glory and the [Spirit] of the God upon you rests" (το της δοξης και το του θεου πνευμα εφ υμας αναπαυεται). Some later manuscripts add "on their part he is blasphemed, but on your part he is glorified" — this addition is absent from Sinaiticus. EMET follows Sinaiticus in omitting it.

8. **4:16** — Sinaiticus reads "in this name" (εν τω ονοματι τουτω) rather than "in this part/matter" (εν τω μερει τουτω) found in some witnesses. This is a notable Sinaiticus variant.

9. **4:17** — Sinaiticus reads "for the judgment to begin from the house of the God" (του αρξασθαι το κριμα απο του οικου του θεου). The definite article with θεου is characteristic of Sinaiticus in this passage.

10. **4:18** — This verse quotes Proverbs 11:31 (LXX). Sinaiticus preserves the Septuagint form: "if the righteous one is scarcely saved" (ει ο δικαιος μολις σωζεται).

11. **4:19** — Sinaiticus reads "the souls of them" (τας ψυχας αυτων) rather than "their own souls" (εαυτων). The term "faithful Creator" (πιστω κτιστη) is unique in the New Testament — κτιστης appears only here.
